meanings.definition: make ready or suitable or equip in advance for a particular purpose or for some use, event, etc
examples: Get the children ready for school!
prepare for war
I was fixing to leave town after I paid the hotel bill

meanings.definition: prepare for eating by applying heat
examples: Cook me dinner, please
can you make me an omelette?
fix breakfast for the guests, please

meanings.definition: to prepare verbally, either for written or spoken delivery
examples: prepare a report
prepare a speech

meanings.definition: arrange by systematic planning and united effort
examples: machinate a plot
organize a strike
devise a plan to take over the director's office

meanings.definition: educate for a future role or function
examples: He is grooming his son to become his successor
The prince was prepared to become King one day
They trained him to be a warrior
The old master is training world-class violinists
we develop the leaders for the future

meanings.definition: lead up to and soften by sounding the dissonant note in it as a consonant note in the preceding chord
examples: prepare the discord in bar 139

meanings.definition: undergo training or instruction in preparation for a particular role, function, or profession
examples: She is training to be a teacher
He trained as a legal aid

meanings.definition: made ready or fit or suitable beforehand
examples: a prepared statement
be prepared for emergencies
prepared to take risks

meanings.definition: equipped or prepared with necessary intellectual resources
examples: graduates well equipped to handle such problems
equipped to be a scholar
